user.title: Madrileña Red de Gas
user.description:
This integration receives consumption data over HTTP from a *bookmarklet* (a JavaScript favorite) that you click in your browser while logged into Madrileña's customer portal.

Enter a name for this installation, the HTTPS URL of your Home Assistant, and the distribution parameters (people + climates + outdoor temperature). On confirmation you will see the bookmarklet code ready to copy in a persistent notification.

data.name: Installation name
data.people: Number of people in the household
data.outdoor_temp_entity: Outdoor temperature sensor or weather entity
data.enable_cost: Compute price (€) on top of consumption (m³)

data_description.name: Human-readable label for your sensors and device (e.g. "Main house"). The meter number remains the internal identifier and gets bound automatically the first time you click the bookmarklet.
data_description.ha_url: Where the bookmarklet POSTs to your HA. Can be a local HTTPS URL (LAN IP or hostname) or public (Nabu Casa, DuckDNS, Cloudflare Tunnel…). It must be HTTPS — browsers block fetch HTTP from the portal (HTTPS). Default: {default_url}.
data_description.people: Used to estimate DHW (domestic hot water) consumption. The more people, the more m³/day are assigned to DHW and the less to heating. If you live alone, set 1.
data_description.climate_entities: Pick one or more `climate.*` (thermostats) and/or `binary_sensor.*` (demand sensors like Airzone "floor demand"). Climates are counted while `hvac_action == heating`; binary_sensors while `on`. For Airzone-style setups that mix gas underfloor + electric A/C, the floor binary_sensor is the cleanest signal. If none selected, distribution is done only by outdoor temperature (HDD).
data_description.outdoor_temp_entity: Any sensor.* of temperature or weather.* (e.g. weather.met_no by default). For days before HA's recorder history, Open-Meteo Archive (free, no API key) is used as a complement. If left empty, Open-Meteo is always used.
data_description.hdd_base_c: Temperature below which heating is considered active. 18 °C is the Spanish standard (REE / IDAE). Only lower it if your house is very cold or you know what you're doing.
data_description.enable_cost: If enabled, extra cost entities (€ accumulated, kWh accumulated) will be created and can be added to the Energy panel. If disabled, only m³ entities.

cost.title: Cost parameters
cost.description: Madrileña is only the distributor; the gas price is set by your retailer (Endesa, Naturgy, TUR…). These two values are used to convert m³ to kWh and then to €.

data_description.kwh_per_m3: Higher Heating Value. In Spain Madrileña's average is around 11.70 kWh/m³ but varies ±2 % per quarter and per zone. It appears on your gas bill as "Conversion coefficient".
data_description.price_eur_kwh: Unit price applied to the consumed energy. Find it on your last bill as "Consumed energy" divided by the kWh billed. Update it when you renegotiate your tariff.

error.missing_ha_url: An HTTPS URL is required so the bookmarklet can POST from your browser.
error.invalid_ha_url: The URL must start with http:// or https://. HTTPS recommended — browsers block fetch HTTP from the portal.

abort.reauth_not_supported: There is no HA session to expire — data is supplied by your browser via the bookmarklet. If you need to rotate the token, delete this integration and add it again.

init.title: Madrileña Red de Gas
init.description: Edit the distribution and cost parameters. Changes are applied on the next coordinator refresh (1 h or when a new reading arrives).

data.people: Number of people in the household
data.outdoor_temp_entity: Outdoor temperature sensor or weather entity
data.enable_cost: Compute price (€) on top of consumption (m³)

data_description.people: Change this when someone moves in or out. DHW consumption is recalculated backwards on the next refresh.
data_description.climate_entities: Empty = distribution by HDD only. Accepts `climate.*` (reads `hvac_action`) and `binary_sensor.*` (reads `on`/`off`). For Airzone gas+electric setups, `binary_sensor.*demanda_de_suelo*` isolates gas best. Days without history still get HDD-only weight.
data_description.outdoor_temp_entity: Empty = Open-Meteo Archive is always used. Useful if you have an outdoor probe you prefer over a generic weather entity.
data_description.hdd_base_c: Below this temperature counts as a cold day. Standard 18 °C.
data_description.acs_m3_per_person_day: By default the integration deduces this value from your summer periods (when gas is only used for hot water). If your usage is atypical (gym away, long vacations), put your preferred value here. Empty or 0 = automatic.
data_description.enable_cost: If you disable it, the cost entities disappear when the integration reloads (the m³ sensors continue as before).
data_description.kwh_per_m3: Appears on your gas bill as "Conversion coefficient". Spain ~11.70.
data_description.price_eur_kwh: Find it on your last bill. Change it when you renegotiate your tariff.

refresh.name: Refresh sensors
refresh.description: Reprocesses the local cache and refreshes attributes. Does NOT download anything from the portal — for that you must click the bookmarklet in the browser.

instance.name: Installation
instance.description: Name or entry_id of a specific integration. Empty = all.

show_bookmarklet.name: Show bookmarklet
show_bookmarklet.description: Re-displays the bookmarklet code in a persistent notification — useful if you closed it by accident.

instance.name: Installation
instance.description: Name or entry_id of the integration whose bookmarklet you want to see.
